source_pr.title: ddl: make TestCreateTableWithRangeColumnPartition stable
source_pr.body:

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/26986 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:

How it Works:
add `order by`.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory


symptoms: tk.MustQuery(`select * from t where a < X'0D' order by a`).Check(testkit.Rows("\x0B", "\x0C")) (ddl/db_partition_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery(`select * from t where a < X'0D' order by a`).Check(testkit.Rows("\x0B", "\x0C")) (ddl/db_partition_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to non-deterministic row ordering when checking query results without a stable ordering guarantee. This PR stabilizes it by adding an explicit `ORDER BY` clause so the test assertions observe deterministic output ordering.
fix_pattern: Add explicit ORDER BY in test queries where result order is asserted.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery(`select * from t where a < X'0D' order by a`).Check(testkit.Rows("\x0B", "\x0C")) (ddl/db_partition_test.go)
